sections.title: 0. Setup: f = 7 pieces, K_5 interior adjacency

tests.name: f(P) = 7 and boundary-per-piece = 3 gives interior-per-piece = 4
5 pieces x 4 interior faces / 2 = |E(K_5)| = 10

sections.title: 1. Singleton Sym(P)-orbits on interior faces fail on K_5

tests.name: Orbit structures on 4 interior faces with AT LEAST ONE singleton: (1,1,1,1), (2,1,1), (3,1)
Such orbits enforce a perfect matching on 5 pieces via the singleton label, which is impossible (5 is odd)
Singleton-orbit structures (1,1,1,1), (2,1,1), (3,1) are therefore EXCLUDED by CTL on K_5
tests.detail: no perfect matching on an odd vertex set

sections.title: 2. Four multisets with distinct boundary areas -> Sym(P) trivial; active accidental-even residual remains

tests.name: Number of multisets with 3 distinct boundary areas: 4
Number of multisets with a repeated boundary area: 1
A rigid motion fixing 3 non-parallel planes through v_P must be the identity
tests.detail: 3 non-parallel planes intersect only at v_P, and the only rigid motion fixing each of 3 such planes as a set is identity

tests.name: Multiset (6, 12, 30): Sym(P) = trivial; previous orbit-transport audit gives orbit structure (1,1,1,1), while active accidental-even metric coincidences remain residual
Multiset (6, 15, 27): Sym(P) = trivial; previous orbit-transport audit gives orbit structure (1,1,1,1), while active accidental-even metric coincidences remain residual
Multiset (6, 18, 24): Sym(P) = trivial; previous orbit-transport audit gives orbit structure (1,1,1,1), while active accidental-even metric coincidences remain residual
Multiset (12, 16, 20): Sym(P) = trivial; previous orbit-transport audit gives orbit structure (1,1,1,1), while active accidental-even metric coincidences remain residual

sections.title: 3. Multiset (12, 12, 24): Sym(P) = C_2 at most; rules out orbit (4)

tests.name: Multiset (12, 12, 24) has one repeated area (12, 12)
Sym(P) for (12,12,24) satisfies |Sym(P)| in {1, 2}
tests.detail: bisector-plane reflection sigma is the unique non-trivial candidate

tests.name: Orbit structure (4) requires |Sym(P)| >= 4: IMPOSSIBLE for (12,12,24)
Only candidate orbit structure for (12, 12, 24): (2, 2)
tests.detail: other singleton-containing structures are killed by Section 1; (4) is killed above

sections.title: 4. Orbit structure (4) is impossible for every multiset

tests.name: Multiset (6, 12, 30): max |Sym(P)| = 1 < 4  =>  orbit (4) IMPOSSIBLE
Multiset (6, 15, 27): max |Sym(P)| = 1 < 4  =>  orbit (4) IMPOSSIBLE
Multiset (6, 18, 24): max |Sym(P)| = 1 < 4  =>  orbit (4) IMPOSSIBLE
Multiset (12, 12, 24): max |Sym(P)| = 2 < 4  =>  orbit (4) IMPOSSIBLE
Multiset (12, 16, 20): max |Sym(P)| = 1 < 4  =>  orbit (4) IMPOSSIBLE

sections.title: 5. Orbit (2,2) on K_5: triangle cycle-consistency yields contradiction

tests.name: A-cycle has 5 edges
B-cycle has 5 edges
A-cycle and B-cycle are edge-disjoint and cover K_5
A-cycle is 2-regular on 5 vertices
B-cycle is 2-regular on 5 vertices
Exhaustive enumeration: 0/1024 assignments satisfy all 10 K_5-triangle constraints
Every triangle of K_5 contributes a distilled constraint z_{i'} + z_{j'} == 1 on a 2-subset of {1,...,5}
The 10 triangles of K_5 cover ALL 10 pairs in {1,...,5} via these distilled constraints
Constraints demand z_i != z_j for all 10 pairs, but z_i in {0,1}: pigeonhole yields contradiction
tests.detail: 5 pairwise-distinct values impossible in a 2-element set

tests.name: Orbit structure (2, 2) on K_5 is therefore IMPOSSIBLE

sections.title: 6. Synthesis: f = 7 audit and active residual

tests.name: All 5 surviving multisets are impossible only in the previous auxiliary orbit-transport closure
Active public synthesis supersedes the previous auxiliary closure and leaves only the accidental-even metric residual open

cases.name: multiset_(6, 12, 30)
cases.reason: Three distinct boundary areas force Sym(P) = 1.  Under the previous auxiliary orbit-transport upgrade, congruent interior facets would have to lie in singleton Sym(P)-orbits, producing a perfect matching on 5 pieces, impossible on an odd vertex set.  The current ledger does not make that upgrade for accidental even metric coincidences, so this multiset remains within the single accidental-even metric residual scope.
cases.reason_type: previous auxiliary orbit-transport + CTL matching; active residual open
cases.conditional_on: previous auxiliary orbit-detection assumption; active public stack leaves accidental-even metric residual open

cases.name: multiset_(12, 12, 24)
cases.reason: Sym(P) <= C_2 rules out a genuine symmetry orbit (4), and the genuine (2,2) orbit branch is killed by the orientation obstruction.  Singleton/odd metric classes are killed by parity. What is not excluded here is the current active accidental-even metric residual, where metric congruences are not supplied by a Sym(P)-orbit transport.
cases.reason_type: previous auxiliary orbit-transport + orientation certificate; active residual open
cases.conditional_on: previous auxiliary orbit-transport assumption; active public stack leaves accidental-even metric residual open

conclusion.active_public_status: not fully excluded; accidental-even metric residual remains open in phase26e synthesis
conclusion.previous_auxiliary_scope: closure under previous auxiliary orbit-transport assumptions only
conclusion.active_public_scope: accidental-even metric residual remains open for these f=7 multisets
